meaning: adj.资金短缺的,财力不足的
examples: From the beginning, the project was underfinanced.
Others point to an underfinanced pension system so skewed in favor of older Japanese that many younger workers simply refuse to pay;
Under such a system, underdogs and underfinanced challengers would have an opportunity to gain traction against establishment favorites.
Notably, the GOP Iowa caucuses were won not by one of the big early juggernaut candidates, but by the underfinanced Mike Huckabee.
ARKO, which is underfinanced, inexpert and many months late in starting work.
